2026-02-28.en:
# ASCO GU 2026 Conference Summary - Final Day (February 28, 2026)

Based on the available tweets from the final day of ASCO GU 2026, here are the key takeaways:

• **Conference Conclusion with Practice-Changing Science**: ASCO GU 2026 concluded with presentations of practice-changing scientific findings and clinical takeaways, emphasizing the translational impact of the research presented throughout the conference week. [Tweet](https://x.com/ASCO/status/2027760844608610716)

• **Emphasis on Collaborative Networks**: The conference organizers highlighted the importance of maintaining professional connections established during the meeting to foster ongoing collaboration in genitourinary oncology research and clinical practice. [Tweet](https://x.com/ASCO/status/2027760844608610716)

• **Conference Services and Resources**: Final opportunities were provided for attendees to access ASCO support services, obtain conference materials, and learn about upcoming ASCO initiatives including ASCO Breakthrough in Singapore, demonstrating the organization's commitment to global oncology education. [Tweet](https://x.com/ASCO/status/2027768340328980743)

*Note: The available tweets focus primarily on conference logistics and closing activities rather than specific clinical data presentations. Detailed scientific content and expert clinical insights would require access to additional conference communications or presentation abstracts.*
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Dia Final (28/02/2026)

Com base nos tweets disponíveis do último dia do ASCO GU 2026, observa-se o encerramento do congresso com foco na consolidação dos aprendizados:

• **Encerramento com ciência transformadora**: O congresso finalizou apresentando avanços científicos com potencial de mudança na prática clínica em oncologia genitourinária, consolidando os principais insights clínicos da semana ([Tweet](https://x.com/ASCO/status/2027760844608610716))

• **Estímulo à colaboração continuada**: A ASCO enfatizou a importância da manutenção de redes de colaboração entre os participantes, incentivando o intercâmbio de informações de contato para dar continuidade às discussões científicas iniciadas durante o evento ([Tweet](https://x.com/ASCO/status/2027760844608610716))

• **Busca por momentos de descoberta clínica**: O congresso solicitou feedback dos participantes sobre seus principais "momentos eureka" da semana, indicando a presença de descobertas significativas que impactaram a comunidade médica presente ([Tweet](https://x.com/ASCO/status/2027760844608610716))

• **Expansão internacional da ASCO**: Foi destacada a iniciativa ASCO Breakthrough em Singapura, demonstrando o compromisso da organização com a disseminação global do conhecimento em oncologia ([Tweet](https://x.com/ASCO/status/2027768340328980743))

*Nota: Os tweets analisados focaram nos aspectos organizacionais do encerramento. Para insights clínicos específicos, seria necessário acesso a tweets com conteúdo científico detalhado das apresentações.*

2026-02-28.en:
# ASCO GU 2026 Conference Summary - February 28, 2026

## Key Clinical Insights from Bladder Cancer Research

• **Breakthrough in Perioperative Bladder Cancer Treatment**: The KEYNOTE-B15/EV-304 trial demonstrated superior efficacy of perioperative enfortumab vedotin plus pembrolizumab compared to standard cisplatin plus gemcitabine in patients with muscle-invasive bladder cancer, showing significant improvements across multiple endpoints including event-free survival, overall survival, and pathological complete response rates. [Tweet](https://x.com/ASCOPost/status/2027764530327994438)

• **Clinically Meaningful Survival Benefit**: The combination immunotherapy regimen achieved a 24-month event-free survival rate of 79.4% versus 66.2% for chemotherapy, representing a substantial 47% reduction in risk of disease progression or death (HR = 0.53). [Tweet](https://x.com/ASCOPost/status/2027764530327994438)

• **Expert Presentation on Novel Therapeutic Approach**: Dr. Matthew Galsky presented these pivotal results, highlighting the potential paradigm shift toward antibody-drug conjugate and immunotherapy combinations in the perioperative setting for muscle-invasive bladder cancer patients. [Tweet](https://x.com/ASCOPost/status/2027764530327994438)

• **Practice-Changing Implications**: These findings suggest that the enfortumab vedotin plus pembrolizumab combination may establish a new standard of care for perioperative treatment of muscle-invasive bladder cancer, potentially replacing traditional platinum-based chemotherapy regimens. [Tweet](https://x.com/ASCOPost/status/2027764530327994438)
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Principais Destaques

## Câncer de Bexiga Músculo-Invasivo

• **Avanço significativo no tratamento perioperatório**: O estudo KEYNOTE-B15/EV-304 demonstrou superioridade da combinação enfortumab vedotina + pembrolizumab comparado ao esquema padrão cisplatina + gencitabina em pacientes com câncer de bexiga músculo-invasivo, com melhora substancial na sobrevida livre de eventos (SLE de 24 meses: 79,4% vs 66,2%, HR = 0,53). [Link do tweet](https://x.com/ASCOPost/status/2027764530327994438)

• **Benefício triplo em desfechos oncológicos**: A terapia combinada com imunoterapia e conjugado anticorpo-droga mostrou melhora simultânea em três endpoints críticos - sobrevida livre de eventos (EFS), sobrevida global (OS) e resposta patológica completa (pCR) - representando um marco no tratamento multimodal do câncer de bexiga. [Link do tweet](https://x.com/ASCOPost/status/2027764530327994438)

• **Redução significativa do risco**: A redução de 47% no risco de eventos (HR = 0,53) observada com o regime experimental sugere um potencial para mudança do paradigma terapêutico no cenário perioperatório, especialmente considerando o perfil de eficácia robusto apresentado pelo Dr. Matthew Galsky. [Link do tweet](https://x.com/ASCOPost/status/2027764530327994438)

2026-02-28.en:
Based on Bradley McGregor's tweet from ASCO GU 2026, here is a summary of the key clinical content:

• **Novel Adjuvant Combination Trial in Renal Cell Carcinoma**: The STRIKE trial (A032201) is actively enrolling patients through the ALLIANCE cooperative group to investigate the combination of tivozanib (a VEGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor) with pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1 immunotherapy) in the adjuvant setting following surgical resection of clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC). [Tweet](https://x.com/BradMcG04/status/2027764474028126327)

• **Strategic Treatment Sequencing Approach**: This study represents an important exploration of dual-pathway inhibition (anti-angiogenic plus immunotherapy) in the post-surgical setting, potentially addressing the need for effective adjuvant therapies to prevent recurrence in high-risk ccRCC patients. [Tweet](https://x.com/BradMcG04/status/2027764474028126327)

• **Cooperative Group Infrastructure**: The trial is being conducted through the established ALLIANCE oncology cooperative group network, ensuring robust patient accrual and standardized protocols across multiple institutions for this adjuvant renal cancer study. [Tweet](https://x.com/BradMcG04/status/2027764474028126327)

• **Conference Presentation Format**: The research was presented as a Trial in Progress (TIP) at ASCO GU 2026, indicating this is an ongoing study in the enrollment phase rather than presenting efficacy or safety outcomes. [Tweet](https://x.com/BradMcG04/status/2027764474028126327)
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Bradley McGregor (@BradMcG04)

## Principais Destaques Clínicos:

• **Novo Estudo STRIKE (A032201) em Recrutamento**: O estudo clínico conduzido pela ALLIANCE está investigando a combinação de Tivozanib com pembrolizumab como terapia adjuvante após ressecção cirúrgica de carcinoma de células renais de células claras (ccRCC), representando uma nova abordagem terapêutica no cenário pós-operatório. [Tweet](https://x.com/BradMcG04/status/2027764474028126327)

• **Estratégia Terapêutica Inovadora**: A combinação de um inibidor de tirosina quinase (Tivozanib) com imunoterapia (pembrolizumab) no contexto adjuvante pode representar um avanço significativo no tratamento de pacientes com ccRCC ressecado, explorando sinergias entre terapia antiangiogênica e imunomodulação.

• **Apresentação no ASCO GU 2026**: O estudo foi apresentado como TIP (Trial in Progress) durante o congresso, indicando que se encontra em fase ativa de recrutamento e representa uma das investigações clínicas de destaque no evento.

• **Colaboração Institucional**: O envolvimento da ALLIANCE e do Dana-Farber Cancer Institute demonstra a natureza colaborativa multicêntrica do estudo, potencializando o recrutamento e a qualidade dos dados coletados.

2026-02-28.en:
# Dana-Farber ASCO GU 2026 Summary

Based on the available tweet from Dana-Farber News, here are the key clinical highlights:

• **STRIKE! Trial Update in High-Risk RCC**: Dr. Bradley McGregor from Dana-Farber presented updated results from the STRIKE! clinical trial, investigating a short-term intensified combination regimen of pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1 immunotherapy) and tivozanib (VEGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor) specifically for patients with high-risk renal cell carcinoma. [Tweet](https://x.com/DanaFarberNews/status/2027738058271961255)

• **Novel Combination Approach**: The trial represents an innovative therapeutic strategy combining checkpoint inhibition with targeted VEGFR inhibition in an intensified, time-limited treatment paradigm for high-risk RCC patients, potentially offering improved efficacy while limiting long-term treatment burden.

• **Focus on High-Risk Population**: The study specifically targets high-risk renal cell carcinoma patients, addressing a critical patient population that requires more aggressive therapeutic interventions and represents an area of significant unmet medical need in genitourinary oncology.

*Note: This summary is based on limited available information from a single tweet. Additional clinical data, efficacy outcomes, safety profiles, and detailed trial results would require access to the full conference presentation or additional reporting.*
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Dana-Farber Cancer Institute

## Principais Destaques Clínicos

• **Estudo STRIKE! em Carcinoma de Células Renais de Alto Risco**: O Dr. Brad McGregor (@BradMcG04) do Dana-Farber apresentou atualizações sobre o ensaio clínico STRIKE!, que investiga uma abordagem de intensificação de curto prazo combinando pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1) com tivozanib (inibidor de tirosina quinase multi-alvo) para pacientes com carcinoma de células renais de alto risco. [Tweet](https://x.com/DanaFarberNews/status/2027738058271961255)

• **Estratégia Terapêutica Inovadora**: O protocolo STRIKE! representa uma abordagem diferenciada ao utilizar intensificação de curto prazo da imunoterapia combinada com terapia-alvo, potencialmente oferecendo uma alternativa aos regimes de tratamento prolongado convencionais em RCC de alto risco. [Tweet](https://x.com/DanaFarberNews/status/2027738058271961255)

• **Foco em População de Alto Risco**: O estudo direciona-se especificamente para pacientes com carcinoma de células renais de alto risco, uma população que tradicionalmente apresenta prognóstico reservado e necessita de estratégias terapêuticas mais agressivas e eficazes. [Tweet](https://x.com/DanaFarberNews/status/2027738058271961255)

2026-02-28.en:
# ASCO GU 2026 Bladder Cancer Updates - Summary by Dr. Toni Choueiri

• **Bladder-sparing strategy shows promise**: A novel approach using TURBT followed by 2 cycles of pembrolizumab achieved 43% complete clinical response (cCR) rates, with patients achieving cCR able to omit upfront radical cystectomy and continue pembrolizumab maintenance. Undetectable early ctDNA emerged as a strong predictor of favorable outcomes. [Tweet](https://x.com/DrChoueiri/status/2027544670914678996)

• **SunRISe-2 trial fails to meet primary endpoint**: The combination of Gem-iDRS plus cetrelimab did not improve bladder-intact event-free survival compared to chemoradiotherapy (CRT) in muscle-invasive bladder cancer, with HR 1.71 and 24-month bladder-intact EFS of 58.5% vs 66.5% for CRT. [Tweet](https://x.com/DrChoueiri/status/2027537855762075703)

• **KEYNOTE-905 demonstrates superior perioperative efficacy**: Enfortumab vedotin plus pembrolizumab (EV+pembro) showed impressive results with pathological complete response rates of 57.1% vs 8.6% and pathological downstaging of 65.9% vs 12.6%. Disease-free survival significantly favored the combination therapy (HR 0.37) with median DFS not reached versus 23.6 months for control. [Tweet](https://x.com/DrChoueiri/status/2027540735252435060)

• **Perioperative immunotherapy combinations continue to establish dominance** in muscle-invasive bladder cancer treatment paradigms, with EV+pembrolizumab emerging as a particularly effective regimen based on the KEYNOTE-905 final readout data.
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Câncer de Bexiga: Insights do Dr. Toni Choueiri

• **Estratégia de preservação vesical promissora**: Apresentação de @JonAnker1 demonstrou protocolo inovador com TURBT seguido de 2 ciclos de pembrolizumab e reestadimento, alcançando 43% de resposta completa clínica (cCR). Pacientes com cCR evitaram cistectomia radical inicial e continuaram pembrolizumab, com ctDNA indetectável sinalizando desfechos altamente favoráveis. [Link](https://x.com/DrChoueiri/status/2027544670914678996)

• **KEYNOTE-905 confirma superioridade do EV+pembrolizumab perioperatório**: Dados apresentados por #DrAndersUllen mostraram taxa de resposta patológica completa (pCR) impressionante de 57,1% vs 8,6% no controle, e resposta de downstaging patológico (pDS) de 65,9% vs 12,6%. A sobrevida livre de doença favoreceu significativamente EV+pembrolizumab (HR 0,37) com mediana não atingida vs 23,6 meses. [Link](https://x.com/DrChoueiri/status/2027540735252435060)

• **SunRISe-2 falha em demonstrar benefício**: O estudo final apresentado por @AndreaNecchi comparando Gem-iDRS + cetrelimab vs quimiorradioterapia (CRT) em câncer de bexiga músculo-invasivo NÃO melhorou a sobrevida livre de eventos com bexiga intacta (HR 1,71), com taxas de 24 meses de 58,5% vs 66,5% favorecendo CRT. [Link](https://x.com/DrChoueiri/status/2027537855762075703)

• **Consolidação do EV+pembrolizumab como novo padrão**: Os dados reforçam a combinação enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab como estratégia altamente eficaz no cenário perioperatório, estabelecendo-se como uma das principais opções terapêuticas para câncer de bexiga avançado.

2026-02-28.en:
Based on the provided tweet from Daniel E Spratt (@DrSpratticus), here is the conference summary:

## ASCO GU 2026 Summary - Dr. Daniel E Spratt

• **Primary Care Integration in Oncology**: Dr. Spratt emphasized the critical role of primary care physicians as "unsung heroes" in healthcare delivery, highlighting the importance of multidisciplinary collaboration between primary care and oncology specialties. [Tweet](https://x.com/DrSpratticus/status/2027726731935711556)

• **Therapeutic Radiation Advances**: Presentation focused on significant progress in therapeutic radiation techniques for prostate cancer management, suggesting continued evolution in radiation oncology approaches and treatment protocols. [Tweet](https://x.com/DrSpratticus/status/2027726731935711556)

• **Educational Outreach to Primary Care**: The symposium represents efforts to bridge knowledge gaps between specialized oncology care and primary care practice, potentially improving early detection and patient care coordination in prostate cancer management. [Tweet](https://x.com/DrSpratticus/status/2027726731935711556)

*Note: This summary is based on limited tweet content. Additional clinical data, specific therapeutic advances, and detailed conference insights were not available in the provided material.*
2026-02-28.pt:
Com base no tweet fornecido, não é possível gerar um resumo adequado do ASCO GU 2026, pois há algumas inconsistências nos dados:

## Limitações da Análise

• **Discrepância temporal**: O tweet é datado de 2026-02-28, mas o ASCO GU (Simpósio Genitourinário da ASCO) tradicionalmente ocorre em janeiro/fevereiro, e o conteúdo do tweet refere-se ao "UH Primary Care Institute Wellness Symposium", não ao ASCO GU

• **Conteúdo não relacionado ao ASCO GU**: O tweet menciona uma apresentação sobre progressos em radioterapia para câncer de próstata em um simpósio de cuidados primários, não especificamente dados ou insights do congresso ASCO GU [Link](https://x.com/DrSpratticus/status/2027726731935711556)

• **Ausência de dados clínicos específicos**: Não há informações sobre estudos, resultados de ensaios clínicos, ou dados apresentados no ASCO GU 2026

## Recomendação

Para uma análise adequada do ASCO GU 2026, seriam necessários tweets que contenham:
- Dados de estudos clínicos apresentados no congresso
- Resultados de ensaios em oncologia genitourinária
- Discussões sobre guidelines e práticas clínicas
- Insights específicos sobre tratamentos em câncer urológico

2026-02-28.en:
# ASCO GU 2026 Conference Summary - Matt Galsky (@MattGalsky)

Based on tweets from February 28, 2026:

• **First-in-Human PPARG Inverse Agonist Study**: A novel therapeutic approach targeting PPARG (peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma) as an inverse agonist is being investigated specifically for luminal subtype urothelial cancer, representing a potentially innovative precision medicine strategy for this molecularly defined patient population. [Tweet](https://x.com/MattGalsky/status/2027772903526482225)

• **Publication in High-Impact Journal**: The PPARG inverse agonist research has been published in Nature Medicine, indicating significant scientific rigor and potential clinical relevance for the genitourinary oncology community. [Tweet](https://x.com/MattGalsky/status/2027772903526482225)

• **Collaborative Research Effort**: The work involves multiple prominent genitourinary oncology experts and institutions, suggesting a multi-center collaborative approach to advancing treatment options for urothelial carcinoma patients. [Tweet](https://x.com/MattGalsky/status/2027772903526482225)

• **Media Recognition**: The research findings received additional visibility through coverage by ASCO Post, highlighting the potential clinical significance of this novel therapeutic approach for the broader oncology community. [Tweet](https://x.com/MattGalsky/status/2027780370759094317)
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Tweets de Matt Galsky (@MattGalsky)

## Principais Destaques Clínicos:

• **Novo alvo terapêutico em câncer urotelial luminal**: Apresentação de dados first-in-human (FIH) de um agonista inverso de PPARG especificamente direcionado para câncer urotelial luminal, representando uma abordagem terapêutica inovadora baseada em subtipos moleculares. A publicação na Nature Medicine indica relevância científica significativa. [Tweet](https://x.com/MattGalsky/status/2027772903526482225)

• **Reconhecimento pela comunidade oncológica**: O trabalho apresentado recebeu destaque do ASCO Post, sugerindo impacto clínico relevante e interesse da comunidade médica especializada em uro-oncologia. [Tweet](https://x.com/MattGalsky/status/2027780370759094317)

• **Colaboração multidisciplinar**: O engajamento de múltiplos especialistas renomados em uro-oncologia (@apolo_andrea, @PGrivasMDPhD, @DrChoueiri) indica colaboração internacional e validação por experts da área.

• **Foco em medicina de precisão**: A abordagem direcionada ao subtipo luminal de câncer urotelial demonstra avanço na estratificação molecular e tratamento personalizado, alinhado com tendências atuais da oncologia moderna.

2026-02-28.en:
# ASCO GU 2026 Conference Summary - February 28, 2026

## Key Clinical Insights and Data Highlights

• **ctDNA as Predictive Biomarker in Bladder Cancer**: The IMvigor011 trial demonstrated that adjuvant atezolizumab enhances circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) clearance in muscle-invasive bladder cancer patients, with ctDNA clearance correlating to improved clinical outcomes, reinforcing ctDNA's role as both a prognostic marker and treatment guide. [Tweet](https://x.com/OncLive/status/2027589533525475675)

• **Bladder-Sparing Treatment Strategy Shows Limited Efficacy**: The SunRISe-2 trial results revealed that the combination of gemcitabine-iDRS plus cetrelimab failed to demonstrate superiority over standard chemoradiotherapy in muscle-invasive bladder cancer, indicating challenges in developing effective bladder-sparing treatment approaches. [Tweet](https://x.com/OncLive/status/2027763815291539825)

• **High-Impact RCC Studies Generate Conference Interest**: Three renal cell carcinoma studies—LITESPARK-011, LITESPARK-022, and K-COMPASS—were identified as generating significant attention and anticipated to drive major clinical discussions at the symposium. [Tweet](https://x.com/OncLive/status/2027764859291533618)

• **Age-Stratified Analysis of Immunotherapy in RCC**: New data examining outcomes with ipilimumab plus nivolumab combination therapy in clear cell renal cell carcinoma were presented with specific focus on efficacy variations according to patient age demographics. [Tweet](https://x.com/OncLive/status/2027572073665532364)
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Principais Destaques Clínicos

• **Biomarcador ctDNA como guia terapêutico**: O estudo IMvigor011 demonstrou que atezolizumabe adjuvante aumenta o clearance de ctDNA, correlacionando-se com melhores desfechos em câncer de bexiga músculo-invasivo, reforçando o papel do ctDNA não apenas como marcador, mas como guia de tratamento. [Link](https://x.com/OncLive/status/2027589533525475675)

• **Estudos em carcinoma renal de células claras (ccRCC) em destaque**: Três estudos principais atraíram atenção nas sessões de RCC - LITESPARK-011, LITESPARK-022 e K-COMPASS - prometendo discussões significativas sobre novas abordagens terapêuticas. [Link](https://x.com/OncLive/status/2027764859291533618)

• **Limitações da terapia poupadora de bexiga**: O estudo SunRISe-2 mostrou que a combinação Gem-iDRS mais cetrelimabe não superou a quimiorradioterapia no tratamento de câncer de bexiga músculo-invasivo, indicando desafios na estratégia de preservação vesical. [Link](https://x.com/OncLive/status/2027763815291539825)

• **Imunoterapia combinada em ccRCC por faixa etária**: Análise dos desfechos com ipilimumabe mais nivolumabe em ccRCC estratificada por idade dos pacientes, fornecendo insights sobre eficácia da imunoterapia combinada em diferentes grupos etários. [Link](https://x.com/OncLive/status/2027572073665532364)

2026-02-28.en:
# ASCO GU 2026 Conference Summary - Day 2-3 Highlights

## Key Clinical Insights and Data

• **SunRISe-2 Trial Results in Bladder Cancer**: The final readout showed that gemcitabine-iDRS plus cetrelimab did NOT improve bladder-intact event-free survival compared to concurrent chemoradiotherapy (CRT) in muscle-invasive bladder cancer patients (HR 1.71, 24-month BI-EFS 58.5% vs 66.5%). CRT remains the standard of care for patients ineligible for or declining cystectomy. [Tweet 1](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027771199246471458) | [Tweet 2](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027770983499895271) | [Tweet 3](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027770654930698546)

• **Circulating Tumor DNA-Guided Therapy in Bladder Cancer**: Dr. Tom Powles presented exploratory analysis data on ctDNA-guided adjuvant atezolizumab in muscle-invasive bladder cancer, highlighting the potential for precision medicine approaches in bladder cancer treatment. [Tweet](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027772856185290901)

• **Novel Biomarker Research in Renal Cell Carcinoma**: Dr. Mustafa Saleh from Dana-Farber presented research on germline platelet polygenic risk scores to predict renal cell carcinoma survival, representing an innovative approach to prognostic stratification in kidney cancer. [Tweet](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027761532566966616)

• **Metaproteomics in Bladder Cancer**: Dr. Alvaro Pinto presented data on metaproteomics-based subtypes in muscle-invasive bladder carcinoma and their association with survival outcomes, demonstrating advances in molecular characterization of bladder tumors. [Tweet](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027772404127437033)

• **Renal Cell Carcinoma Year in Review**: Dr. Brian Rini provided a comprehensive 2025 year-in-review presentation highlighting both real progress and ongoing challenges in kidney cancer treatment, available through the OncoAlert Colloquium platform. [Tweet](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027745671747411982)
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo do ASCO GU 2026 - Análise OncoAlert

## Principais Destaques Clínicos:

• **Resultados Negativos do Estudo SunRISe-2 em Câncer de Bexiga**: O estudo final demonstrou que a combinação Gem-iDRS + cetrelimab não melhorou a sobrevida livre de eventos com bexiga intacta (BI-EFS) em comparação com quimiorradioterapia concorrente (CRT) em pacientes com carcinoma invasivo de músculo da bexiga (MIBC). A CRT permanece como padrão ouro com BI-EFS de 24 meses de 66,5% vs 58,5% (HR 1,71). [Link](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027771199246471458)

• **Análise Exploratória de ctDNA em Câncer de Bexiga**: Apresentação do Dr. Tom Powles sobre o uso de DNA tumoral circulante (ctDNA) para guiar terapia adjuvante com atezolizumab em MIBC, representando avanço na medicina de precisão para tumores urológicos. [Link](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027772856185290901)

• **Biomarcadores Genéticos em Carcinoma Renal**: Dr. Mustafa Saleh do Dana-Farber apresentou dados sobre escore de risco poligênico de plaquetas germinativas para predizer sobrevida em carcinoma de células renais, indicando potencial para estratificação de risco personalizada. [Link](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027761532566966616)

• **Metaproteômica em Câncer de Bexiga**: Dr. Alvaro Pinto apresentou subtipos baseados em metaproteômica em carcinoma de bexiga invasivo de músculo e sua associação com sobrevida, demonstrando aplicação de tecnologias ômicas avançadas na oncologia urológica. [Link](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027772404127437033)

• **Revisão Anual em Carcinoma Renal**: Dr. Brian Rini conduziu apresentação sobre progressos e desafios contínuos no tratamento de carcinoma de células renais em 2025, consolidando os avanços do ano na área. [Link](https://x.com/OncoAlert/status/2027745671747411982)

2026-02-28.en:
Based on Dr. Petros Grivas' tweets from ASCO GU 2026, here are the key clinical insights:

• **Chemoradiotherapy Trial in Muscle-Invasive Bladder Cancer (MIBC)**: A significant trial led by Andrea Necchi demonstrated the feasibility of accruing patients with chemoradiotherapy as the control arm in MIBC. Despite early termination due to futility analysis, the study provided valuable data with excellent results in the control arm, suggesting potential importance of patient selection criteria. [Tweet](https://x.com/PGrivasMDPhD/status/2027677666007257265)

• **Expert Interview on GU Cancer Innovation**: Dr. Grivas conducted an interview with Dr. Rosa Nadal Rios from Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center/UW Medicine, highlighting ongoing innovative research in genitourinary cancers and recognizing emerging talent in the field. [Tweet](https://x.com/PGrivasMDPhD/status/2027664474979885499)

• **Conference Coverage and Collaboration**: Multiple tweets referenced collaborative discussions with other prominent GU oncologists including Matt Galsky, indicating active knowledge sharing and real-time clinical data dissemination at the conference. [Tweet 1](https://x.com/PGrivasMDPhD/status/2027676592571617589), [Tweet 2](https://x.com/PGrivasMDPhD/status/2027673458239541755)

• **Ongoing Clinical Data Presentation**: Additional clinical content was shared throughout the conference, though specific details were not elaborated in the available tweet text, suggesting comprehensive coverage of GU oncology developments. [Tweet 1](https://x.com/PGrivasMDPhD/status/2027675836342800635), [Tweet 2](https://x.com/PGrivasMDPhD/status/2027673034178621728)
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo dos Tweets de Petros Grivas - ASCO GU 2026

## Principais Insights Clínicos:

• **Estudo de Quimiorradioterapia em MIBC**: Grivas destacou um importante ensaio clínico liderado por Andrea Necchi que demonstrou a viabilidade de recrutamento usando quimiorradioterapia como braço controle em câncer de bexiga músculo-invasivo (MIBC). Embora o estudo tenha sido interrompido precocemente por análise de futilidade, forneceu dados relevantes com excelentes resultados no braço controle, levantando questões sobre seleção de pacientes. [Tweet](https://x.com/PGrivasMDPhD/status/2027677666007257265)

• **Destaque em Pesquisa Inovadora**: O especialista realizou uma entrevista com a Dra. Rosa Nadal Rios, oncologista médica do Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center, discutindo cânceres genitourinários e pesquisas inovadoras na área, demonstrando o foco em colaboração científica e disseminação de conhecimento. [Tweet](https://x.com/PGrivasMDPhD/status/2027664474979885499)

• **Cobertura Abrangente do ASCO GU**: Grivas manteve uma presença ativa durante o congresso, compartilhando múltiplas atualizações e referenciando colaborações com outros especialistas renomados como Matt Galsky, indicando discussões sobre desenvolvimentos significativos em oncologia genitourinária. [Tweet 1](https://x.com/PGrivasMDPhD/status/2027676592571617589) | [Tweet 2](https://x.com/PGrivasMDPhD/status/2027673458239541755)

• **Foco em Dados Clínicos Relevantes**: A ênfase nos resultados do braço controle do estudo de quimiorradioterapia sugere importantes implicações para a prática clínica atual, especialmente relacionadas aos critérios de seleção de pacientes para tratamentos conservadores em MIBC.

Based on the available tweet from Dr. Ashish M. Kamat at ASCO GU 2026, here is the summary:

## ASCO GU 2026 Clinical Insights Summary

• **Value in "Negative" Study Results**: Dr. Kamat emphasized that despite a study showing negative primary results, it represents a "treasure trove of data," highlighting the importance of extracting meaningful clinical insights from trials that don't meet their primary endpoints. [Tweet](https://x.com/UroDocAsh/status/2027543294432207278)

• **Collaborative Expert Discussion**: The tweet engaged multiple prominent urologic oncology experts including Dr. Andrea Apolo, Dr. Andrea Necchi, Dr. Tom Powles, and others, suggesting this was a significant study with broad clinical implications warranting expert commentary. [Tweet](https://x.com/UroDocAsh/status/2027543294432207278)

• **Data Mining Opportunities**: The characterization of the study as a "treasure trove" suggests potential for secondary analyses, biomarker discoveries, or subset analyses that could inform future clinical practice and trial design in urologic oncology. [Tweet](https://x.com/UroDocAsh/status/2027543294432207278)

*Note: This summary is based on limited tweet content. The specific study details, patient population, and clinical outcomes were not provided in the available data.*

## Resumo - ASCO GU 2026 (28/02/2026)

• **Valorização de estudos "negativos"**: Dr. Kamat enfatiza que mesmo estudos com resultados primários negativos podem representar uma "mina de ouro de dados" ("treasure trove of data"), destacando a importância científica de resultados não-positivos para o avanço do conhecimento em uro-oncologia. • **Perspectiva educacional**: A abordagem do Dr. Kamat reflete a importância de extrair valor científico e clínico de todos os tipos de resultados de estudos, independentemente de atingirem seus endpoints primários, contribuindo para o aprendizado coletivo da comunidade oncológica. 2026-02-28.en:
# ASCO GU 2026 Day 2 Summary - Bladder Cancer Advances

Based on tweets from Cristiane D Bergerot, PhD, FASCO (@crisbergerot):

• **Perioperative combination therapy shows promise in muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC)**: The KEYNOTE-B15/EV-304 trial demonstrated strong efficacy for enfortumab vedotin plus pembrolizumab in the perioperative setting for cisplatin-eligible MIBC patients, representing a significant advancement in treatment approach. [Tweet](https://x.com/crisbergerot/status/2027611919931346965)

• **HER2-targeted therapy expands treatment options**: Disitamab vedotin showed efficacy in HER2-expressing advanced urothelial carcinoma (aUC) patients regardless of HER2 expression levels (high or low), potentially broadening the patient population that could benefit from HER2-targeted approaches. [Tweet](https://x.com/crisbergerot/status/2027611919931346965)

• **Precision medicine integration through liquid biopsy**: The IMvigor011 study highlighted the potential role of circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) in guiding adjuvant immunotherapy decisions, demonstrating progress toward personalized treatment strategies in bladder cancer management. [Tweet](https://x.com/crisbergerot/status/2027611919931346965)

• **Rapid evolution of bladder cancer therapeutics**: The collective data presented reflects the accelerating pace of innovation in bladder cancer treatment, with multiple novel approaches showing clinical promise across different disease stages and molecular subtypes. [Tweet](https://x.com/crisbergerot/status/2027611919931346965)
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Dia 2
## Análise baseada nos tweets de Cristiane D Bergerot, PhD, FASCO

• **Avanços significativos no tratamento perioperatório de câncer de bexiga músculo-invasivo**: O estudo KEYNOTE-B15/EV-304 demonstrou resultados promissores com a combinação de enfortumab vedotina (EV) + pembrolizumab em pacientes elegíveis para cisplatina com MIBC, representando uma evolução importante no manejo perioperatório desta condição. [Link](https://x.com/crisbergerot/status/2027611919931346965)

• **Eficácia do disitamab vedotina independente do nível de expressão de HER2**: Os dados apresentados mostraram que o disitamab vedotina demonstrou atividade clínica em carcinoma urotelial avançado com expressão de HER2, independentemente dos níveis altos ou baixos de expressão, expandindo potencialmente a população de pacientes beneficiários. [Link](https://x.com/crisbergerot/status/2027611919931346965)

• **DNA tumoral circulante como biomarcador para terapia adjuvante**: O estudo IMvigor011 destacou o papel do ctDNA como ferramenta de orientação para imunoterapia adjuvante, representando um avanço na medicina de precisão para o tratamento do câncer de bexiga. [Link](https://x.com/crisbergerot/status/2027611919931346965)

• **Evolução acelerada do panorama terapêutico em câncer de bexiga**: A especialista enfatizou a rápida evolução das opções de tratamento para câncer de bexiga, refletindo o progresso significativo na área oncológica com múltiplas inovações terapêuticas sendo apresentadas simultaneamente. [Link](https://x.com/crisbergerot/status/2027611919931346965)

2026-02-28.en:
# ASCO GU 2026 Conference Summary - February 28, 2026

Based on tweets from Dr. Shilpa Gupta (@shilpaonc):

• **SunRise 2 Trial Results in Muscle-Invasive Bladder Cancer (MIBC)**: The pragmatic trial comparing gemcitabine-iDRS plus cetrelimab versus concurrent chemoradiotherapy (CRT) failed to demonstrate improvement in bladder intact event-free survival (BI-EFS) compared to CRT, leading to early trial termination. [Tweet](https://x.com/shilpaonc/status/2027540403436900859)

• **Novel Trial Design**: This represents the first clinical trial in MIBC to include concurrent chemoradiotherapy in the experimental arm, marking a significant methodological approach in bladder cancer research. [Tweet](https://x.com/shilpaonc/status/2027540403436900859)

• **Clinical Practice Implications**: The results reinforce that concurrent chemoradiotherapy continues to be a standard treatment option for muscle-invasive bladder cancer patients, as the investigational immunotherapy combination did not demonstrate superior efficacy. [Tweet](https://x.com/shilpaonc/status/2027540403436900859)

• **Conference Presentation**: The findings were presented by Dr. Andrea Necchi during the rapid oral session at ASCO GU 2026, highlighting the importance of these negative but clinically relevant results for the bladder cancer treatment landscape. [Tweet](https://x.com/shilpaonc/status/2027540403436900859)
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Análise dos Tweets de Shilpa Gupta

## Principais Destaques Clínicos:

• **Estudo SunRise 2 em Câncer de Bexiga Músculo-Invasivo (MIBC)**: O trial pragmático comparando Gencitabina-iDRS + cetrelimab versus radioterapia concomitante (CRT) não demonstrou melhoria na sobrevida livre de eventos bladder-intact (BI-EFS) em relação ao CRT padrão, resultando na interrupção precoce do estudo. [Link do tweet](https://x.com/shilpaonc/status/2027540403436900859)

• **Marco Metodológico em Oncologia Urológica**: Este representa o primeiro ensaio clínico a incluir radioterapia concomitante no braço experimental para MIBC, estabelecendo um precedente importante para futuros estudos comparativos nesta área. [Link do tweet](https://x.com/shilpaonc/status/2027540403436900859)

• **Consolidação do Padrão de Cuidado**: Os resultados reforçam a radioterapia concomitante como padrão ouro mantido para o tratamento de câncer de bexiga músculo-invasivo, demonstrando a dificuldade de superar esta modalidade terapêutica estabelecida. [Link do tweet](https://x.com/shilpaonc/status/2027540403436900859)

• **Apresentação de Destaque no ASCO GU**: A apresentação foi conduzida por Andrea Necchi (@AndreaNecchi) na sessão de apresentações orais rápidas, indicando a relevância clínica significativa destes dados para a comunidade oncológica. [Link do tweet](https://x.com/shilpaonc/status/2027540403436900859)

2026-02-28.en:
# ASCO GU 2026 Key Clinical Insights Summary

• **Bladder-Sparing Approaches Gain Momentum with Novel Biomarker Guidance**: Multiple studies demonstrated promising results for response-adapted bladder preservation strategies, including the SURE-02 trial combining neoadjuvant sacituzumab govitecan with pembrolizumab, and ctDNA-guided approaches in both the IMvigor011 and RETAIN trials showing potential for personalized treatment decisions in muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC). [SURE-02 Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027553183648624926) | [IMvigor011 Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027564529228333209) | [RETAIN Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027723053124194805)

• **HER2-Targeted Therapies Show Expanding Promise Across Bladder Cancer Stages**: Final results from DESTINY-PanTumor02 demonstrated efficacy of trastuzumab deruxtecan in pretreated HER2-expressing bladder cancer, while disitamab vedotin showed activity in both advanced urothelial carcinoma and when combined with perioperative toripalimab in MIBC, suggesting HER2 targeting as a viable therapeutic strategy across disease stages. [DESTINY Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027538082409443579) | [Disitamab Advanced UC Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027545629224779889) | [Disitamab MIBC Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027764564650062136)

• **PSMA-Targeted Radioligand Therapy Optimization in mCRPC**: The randomized phase II OPTIMAL-PSMA trial presented dose optimization strategies for ¹⁷⁷Lu-PSMA-597, comparing dose-intensified versus standard 6-weekly regimens in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer, potentially informing future dosing protocols for this emerging therapeutic modality. [OPTIMAL-PSMA Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027741878020432071)

• **Treatment De-escalation and Personalized Approaches in Prostate Cancer**: The OPTIMAS trial demonstrated feasibility of de-escalation strategies in metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer patients achieving optimal PSA response, while digital pathology-based biomarkers showed validation potential for prognostic stratification, supporting the trend toward precision medicine approaches. [OPTIMAS Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027745755591577772) | [Digital Pathology Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027734373970202896)

• **Novel Intravesical and Combination Therapies for NMIBC**: Emerging data on cretostimogene grenadenorepvec in BCG-naïve high-risk non-muscle invasive bladder cancer (NMIBC) and combination approaches with disitamab vedotin plus electromotive mitomycin-C suggest expanding treatment options for patients with high-risk superficial disease. [Cretostimogene Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027768344598761754) | [Combination NMIBC Tweet](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027549444892139863)
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Principais Destaques Clínicos

• **Terapias Neoadjuvantes e Preservação Vesical**: O estudo SURE-02 demonstrou resultados promissores com sacituzumab govitecan + pembrolizumab neoadjuvante seguido de estratégia adaptada para preservação vesical em MIBC, enquanto análises dos trials RETAIN mostraram o potencial do ctDNA para guiar decisões de preservação vesical adaptada à resposta. [Tweet SURE-02](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027553183648624926) | [Tweet RETAIN](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027723053124194805)

• **Conjugados Anticorpo-Droga (ADCs) em Carcinoma Urotelial**: Múltiplos estudos destacaram a eficácia de ADCs direcionados ao HER2, incluindo trastuzumab deruxtecan (DESTINY-PanTumor02) e disitamab vedotin (RC48G001) em carcinoma urotelial avançado previamente tratado, estabelecendo nova classe terapêutica para tumores HER2-positivos. [Tweet Trastuzumab](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027538082409443579) | [Tweet Disitamab](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027545629224779889)

• **Otimização do PSMA Radioligante**: O trial OPTIMAL-PSMA fase II demonstrou estratégias de intensificação de dose com ¹⁷⁷Lu-PSMA-597 em mCRPC, comparando regimes dose-intensificados versus padrão de 6 semanas, oferecendo insights para personalização da radioterapia com ligantes PSMA. [Tweet OPTIMAL-PSMA](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027741878020432071)

• **Biomarcadores e Medicina de Precisão**: Análises exploratórias do IMvigor011 mostraram dinâmica do ctDNA como guia para atezolizumab adjuvante em MIBC, enquanto validação externa de biomarcadores prognósticos baseados em patologia digital no trial CHHiP demonstrou avanços na estratificação de risco em câncer de próstata. [Tweet IMvigor011](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027564529228333209) | [Tweet CHHiP](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027734373970202896)

• **Estratégias de Desescalonamento em mHSPC**: O estudo OPTIMAS apresentou abordagem descentralizada de desescalonamento em pacientes com mHSPC que alcançaram resposta ótima do PSA, sugerindo potencial para redução da intensidade terapêutica sem comprometer eficácia. [Tweet OPTIMAS](https://x.com/urotoday/status/2027745755591577772)

2026-02-28.en:
# ASCO GU 2026 Summary - Zach Klaassen (@zklaassen_md)

## Key Clinical Insights from February 28, 2026

• **Bladder-Sparing Approach Shows Promise in MIBC**: The HCRN GU 20-444 phase 2 trial demonstrated that pembrolizumab with response-guided bladder preservation achieved a 43% complete clinical response (cCR) rate after TURBT and 2 cycles, with all responding patients opting to avoid upfront radical cystectomy (n=46, median follow-up 11 months). [Tweet](https://x.com/zklaassen_md/status/2027759278187299074)

• **ctDNA Emerges as Predictive Biomarker**: Circulating tumor DNA negativity at cycle 1 showed strong correlation with treatment response, with 90% of non-responders being ctDNA-positive versus only 55% of complete responders being ctDNA-positive. [Tweet](https://x.com/zklaassen_md/status/2027759278187299074)

• **ctDNA Associates with Long-term Outcomes**: The study revealed that ctDNA status was significantly associated with both metastasis-free survival (MFS) and overall survival (OS), suggesting its potential utility as an early prognostic marker in muscle-invasive bladder cancer treated with pembrolizumab. [Tweet](https://x.com/zklaassen_md/status/2027759278187299074)
2026-02-28.pt:
# Resumo ASCO GU 2026 - Análise de Zach Klaassen

## Estudo HCRN GU 20-444: Pembrolizumab com Preservação Vesical Guiada por Resposta em MIBC

• **Taxa de resposta completa clínica promissora**: O estudo fase 2 demonstrou taxa de resposta completa clínica (cCR) de 43% após TURBT e 2 ciclos de pembrolizumab em 46 pacientes com câncer de bexiga músculo-invasivo (MIBC), com todos os respondedores optando por evitar cistectomia radical inicial. [Tweet](https://x.com/zklaassen_md/status/2027759278187299074)

• **DNA tumoral circulante como biomarcador preditivo**: A análise de ctDNA no ciclo 1 mostrou-se altamente preditiva, com 90% dos pacientes sem cCR apresentando ctDNA positivo versus apenas 55% dos pacientes com cCR, estabelecendo associação significativa com sobrevida livre de metástases e sobrevida global. [Tweet](https://x.com/zklaassen_md/status/2027759278187299074)

• **Abordagem de preservação vesical personalizada**: O protocolo response-guided representa avanço na estratégia de preservação vesical, utilizando biomarcadores moleculares para identificar pacientes candidatos ideais ao tratamento conservador com imunoterapia. [Tweet](https://x.com/zklaassen_md/status/2027759278187299074)

• **Seguimento ainda limitado**: Com mediana de follow-up de 11 meses, os dados de eficácia a longo prazo ainda necessitam maturação para estabelecer definitivamente a segurança oncológica desta abordagem em MIBC. [Tweet](https://x.com/zklaassen_md/status/2027759278187299074)
